title: ReconciliationResult
description: Audit artifact for Phase B.5 of Step 8 codegen: static reconciliation between generated test files and the post-extension POMs they import. Records every call site checked and every mismatch (method not found, arity mismatch, parse error) discovered. Persisted to `artifacts/step08/reconcile-result.json` regardless of whether auto-patch succeeded.

test_files_scanned.description: Number of generated test files parsed for `<obj>.<method>(...)` call sites.

fixture_files_scanned.description: Number of distinct fixture files referenced by the plan that were inspected for symbol presence (Phase B.5 fixture check).

fixture_mismatches.description: Plan-declared fixtures that are absent on disk after Phase A4. Each mismatch records the expected file + fixture name + which test_cases referenced it. `fixture_symbol_missing` means the file exists but the named `@pytest.fixture` function is absent (typical race-condition victim). `fixture_file_missing` means the file itself is absent. Empty array means every fixture the plan declared is resolvable.

referenced_by.description: test_case IDs whose `uses_fixtures` declaration referenced this fixture. Surfaced in failure anchors so the human sees which TCs would break at runtime.

existing_symbols.description: All `@pytest.fixture`-decorated function names found in `expected_file` at the time of reconciliation. Empty when `kind == fixture_file_missing` or when the file defines no fixtures.

call_sites_checked.description: Number of POM method invocations resolved through the import manifest and checked against the corresponding POM source on disk.

pom_files_scanned.description: Number of POM source files read from disk to enumerate their actually-defined public methods.

mismatches.description: Every detected discrepancy between a test call site and the resolved POM. Empty array means the generated tests are statically consistent with the post-extension POMs.

kind.description: method_not_found: the called method does not exist on the resolved POM. arity_mismatch: method exists but the call site's positional+keyword arg count is incompatible with the definition. parse_error: the test or POM file could not be parsed and was skipped. likely_typo: the called name is within edit distance of an existing method (see `suggested_method`); auto-patch is intentionally skipped so the human can fix the typo instead of getting a stub that masks the test bug.

test_file.description: SUT-relative path to the generated test file containing the call.

line.description: 1-based line number of the call expression in the test file.

obj_name.description: Name of the object being called (e.g. `login_page`). Resolved through the test file's import manifest to a POM class.

arity.description: Total positional + keyword argument count observed at the call site.

kw_names.description: Names of keyword arguments passed at the call site, in source order. Empty when only positional arguments are used.

snippet.description: One-line source context of the call, used for human-readable error anchors and auto-patch task `purpose` synthesis.

resolved_pom.description: Class name of the POM that `obj_name` resolves to via the test file's imports (e.g. `LoginPage`).

pom_file.description: SUT-relative path to the POM source file scanned for `existing_methods`.

existing_methods.description: All public method names defined on `resolved_pom` at the time of reconciliation. Included verbatim in failure anchors so the agent / human can see what was actually generated.

suggested_method.description: Only populated when `kind == "likely_typo"`: the unique existing method name within `_TYPO_MAX_DIST` (=2) edit distance of `call_site.method_name`. Surface as 'did you mean X?' in the failure anchor.
